name: Camera Angle Classification
description: Classify broadcast camera angles for computer vision training data

category_descriptions.WIDE_CENTER: Main broadcast camera, elevated center, full pitch width, 8+ players
category_descriptions.WIDE_LEFT: Broadcast camera panned to follow left side of pitch
category_descriptions.WIDE_RIGHT: Broadcast camera panned to follow right side of pitch
category_descriptions.MEDIUM: Tighter zone shot, 3-7 players visible, focused on ball area
category_descriptions.CLOSEUP: Tight on 1-2 people — face, celebration, injury, minimal pitch
category_descriptions.BEHIND_GOAL: Camera behind the goal line, goal posts often visible
category_descriptions.AERIAL: Spider cam or steep overhead — bird's eye formations view
category_descriptions.OTHER: Crowd, scoreboard, graphics, tunnel, replay transitions, studio

WIDE_CENTER.description: Main broadcast camera — full pitch width visible, centered. 8+ players visible. This is the standard angle for 60-70% of any broadcast.
WIDE_CENTER.visual_cues: Both goals visible or nearly so. Sideline advertising boards visible at bottom. Players appear small.

WIDE_LEFT.description: Wide camera panning left — same height as center but following play toward the left side of the pitch.
WIDE_LEFT.visual_cues: One goal may be visible while the other is off-screen. Camera is tracking ball movement.

WIDE_RIGHT.description: Wide camera panning right — mirror of WIDE_LEFT.
WIDE_RIGHT.visual_cues: Same as WIDE_LEFT but tracking rightward.

MEDIUM.description: Mid-range shot — 3-6 players visible, focus on a specific area of play (e.g. penalty box, free kick setup).
MEDIUM.visual_cues: Players are medium-sized. Pitch markings clearly visible. Often used during set pieces.

CLOSEUP.description: Close-up — 1-2 players fill the frame. Face, jersey number clearly visible.
CLOSEUP.visual_cues: Individual features visible. Often shows reactions, celebrations, or player close-ups before/after play stops.

BEHIND_GOAL.description: Camera positioned behind/beside the goal — often used for penalties, free kicks near goal, or goal replays.
BEHIND_GOAL.visual_cues: Goal frame visible in foreground. Players approaching the goal. Net may be visible.

AERIAL.description: Bird's-eye / spider-cam — camera positioned directly above the pitch or on cables above.
AERIAL.visual_cues: Players seen from above. Pitch markings form clear geometric patterns. No horizon line.

OTHER.description: Any angle that doesn't fit the above — studio shots, crowd shots, scoreboard graphics, replays with overlays.
OTHER.visual_cues: Non-pitch content. Graphics overlays. Studio setting. Crowd-only shots.

prompt:
Classify this football broadcast screenshot into ONE camera angle.

Categories:
- WIDE_CENTER: Main camera, central elevated position, 8+ players visible, full pitch width
- WIDE_LEFT: Broadcast camera panned left, large pitch area visible
- WIDE_RIGHT: Broadcast camera panned right, large pitch area visible
- MEDIUM: Tighter shot, 3-7 players, focused on ball area
- CLOSEUP: Tight on 1-2 people, face/celebration/injury, minimal pitch visible
- BEHIND_GOAL: Camera behind goal line looking down the pitch, goal posts often visible
- AERIAL: Top-down or steep overhead, spider cam, bird's eye view of formations
- OTHER: Crowd, scoreboard, graphics, tunnel, replay transition, studio

Think step by step:
1. How many players are visible?
2. How much of the pitch is shown (estimate percentage)?
3. What is the camera height and angle?
4. Are there any non-game elements (graphics, replays, studio)?

Respond with JSON only:
{"camera_type": "WIDE_CENTER", "confidence": 0.95, "reasoning": "brief explanation", "players_visible": 18, "pitch_visible_pct": 80, "is_replay": false}

training_data.name: Training Data (RT-DETR / YOLO)
training_data.description: Balanced dataset for object detection model training

tactical_analysis.name: Tactical Analysis
tactical_analysis.description: Maximize wide shots for formation study

quick_test.name: Quick Test
quick_test.description: Small sample to verify setup is working
